enclosures.notes: Syva Low is the LF-extension enclosure of the Syva colinear family (Syva / Syva Low / Syva Sub). It is stackable (Syva mounts on top of Syva Low) and pole/bracket mountable. The current Documentation Center for Syva Low does NOT list a standalone rigging manual; rigging, stacking and mechanical-safety content is contained in the shared family Owner's Manual (Syva_OM_EN.pdf), which covers Syva, Syva Low and Syva Sub together. A legacy standalone 'Syva rigging manual' v1.1 (June 2017) exists only on third-party mirrors (manualslib, eventuryproductions) and has been consolidated into the current OM. Several documents (Amplification Reference, Cables, EASE/GLL-Soundvision, Preset Guide, Weather Protection, White/RAL) are GLOBAL/shared files, not Syva-Low-specific. GLL/Soundvision data for Syva Low ships inside L-Acoustics Soundvision software rather than as a standalone download; the EASE Files zip is the published acoustical-model package. All URLs verified from l-acoustics.com Documentation Center / blob CDN.

5XT = 2-way passive coaxial enclosure (1x 5" cone + 1x 1" compression driver), 3.5 kg, current/active product. Not a flyable/line-array product, so there is NO standalone rigging manual. Mounting is via the ETR5 adjustable U-bracket (2x M6 inserts) plus a 3/8" mic-stand insert; install/mounting guidance is covered inside the Owner's Manual (5XT_OM_EN.pdf), and accessory/mounting kit references appear in the Configurations doc (X_CKL_EN). The Documentation Center serves a single EN Owner's Manual; older standalone User Manuals (5XT_UM_EN v1.0/1.1) exist only on third-party mirrors (promusic.cz, aucop.com, ManualsLib) and have been superseded by the Owner's Manual. GLL/EASE/CATT data are delivered only as global multi-product bundles, not 5XT-specific files. Architect specs are DOCX (mapped to format 'other').

enclosures.notes: DISCONTINUED/LEGACY product. SB218 is a real, distinct L-Acoustics product (dual-18-inch front-loaded vented subwoofer, period companion to V-DOSC and ARCS, usable to 25 Hz at -10 dB). Because it predates the modern product-page CMS it has no standalone l-acoustics.com product page (/archives-product/sb218/ redirects to the homepage) and no hosted product image (blob image paths return 404). The official ARCHIVES bundle (documents.zip) is verified live (HTTP 200, application/octet-stream, 33,316 bytes / 33 kB; listed on l-acoustics.com/archives-product/, EN, ZIP, 33 kB) but contains ONLY one file: SB218_certification_EN.pdf, an EC Declaration of Conformity. It contains NO user manual, rigging manual, or datasheet. The full electroacoustic/physical/amplification/rigging specs come from the official SB218 datasheet (specs SB218 1202, mirrored at av-iq.com and juicesound.co.uk) and the V-DOSC Operator's Manual. NOTE/DISCREPANCY: the official datasheet states 4 ohms; some resale/catalog listings (DigitalResources, McCann) title it '5 ohms / 4400W' - that is a vendor catalog error and 4 ohms is authoritative.

enclosures.notes: DISCONTINUED/LEGACY. The dV-SUB has NO standalone ARCHIVES folder - every casing/encoding of a dV-SUB blob path returns HTTP 404 (control: .../ARCHIVES/SB118/documents.zip returns 200, so the pattern is valid). Its official documentation is bundled inside the dV-DOSC archive: the User Manual is a combined dV-DOSC dV-SUB document and the EC Declaration covers both products. Verified live: HTTP 200/206, Content-Length 24,710,785 bytes (~24.7 MB), application/octet-stream, Last-Modified Fri 01 Nov 2024. The zip contains exactly 3 files: dV_UM_EN_3-0.pdf, dV_UM_FR_3-0.pdf, dV-DOSC_dV-SUB_certification_EN.pdf - there is NO standalone datasheet and NO standalone rigging manual; rigging is an integrated chapter inside the user manual. The official datasheet (rev dVS_SP_EN_1.0/02-09) was not locatable on the L-Acoustics blob (404 on guessed paths) and is included only as a third-party verbatim mirror. dV-DOSC appears on l-acoustics.com/archives-product/; dV-SUB does not appear as its own archive entry.
